fact: The German army fights five months for Stalingrad (roughly August 1942 – February 1943)
A million Soviets surround you — Operation Uranus involved over 1 million Soviet troops
source: Stalingrad, Antony Beevor (1998); Soviet strength figures widely documented

fact: The Luftwaffe will fly in supplies — they deliver a tenth of what you need (needed ~700 tons/day, delivered roughly 85–105 tons/day on average)
source: Stalingrad: The Fateful Siege, Antony Beevor (1998); also Joel Hayward, 'Stopped at Stalingrad: The Luftwaffe and Hitler's Defeat in the East' (1998)

fact: Temperatures dropped to minus thirty degrees (Celsius) during the siege
source: Widely documented; Stalingrad, Antony Beevor (1998) describes temperatures reaching −30°C and below in January 1943

fact: Of 300,000 trapped, roughly 6,000 eventually returned home to Germany (after years in Soviet captivity)
source: Stalingrad, Antony Beevor (1998) — approximately 91,000 surrendered; about 5,000–6,000 survived Soviet POW camps to return to Germany by 1955

fact: Hitler refused to allow a retreat or breakout from the encirclement ('one man wouldn't say retreat')
source: Stalingrad, Antony Beevor (1998); Hitler's no-retreat order to Paulus's 6th Army is widely documented
